Patch Set 1:

this is not the first use of RCU, nor the last one. I think it makes sense to add I'd think it makes sense to add RCU barrier to every module exit.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> Does this mean can we get memory leak errors by accident?

If we won't crash, I don't think we can get memory leak due to this bug, as the deferred free would occur. But then, I believe memory corruption should have occurred if a page has been re-mappped to the same address range used for obd_memory before...

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > Does this mean can we get memory leak errors by accident?
> 
> If we won't crash, I don't think we can get memory leak due to this bug, as the deferred free would occur. But then, I believe memory corruption should have occurred if a page has been re-mappped to the same address range used for obd_memory before...

from obdclass's point of view it would be a leak which testing systems (like Janitor) detect and report as a testing failure.

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> this is not the first use of RCU, nor the last one. I think it makes sense to add I'd think it makes sense to add RCU barrier to every module exit.

Yes, I agree with you Alex, it would be a more generic way to fix rather than the need to decorrelate the OBD_FREE_PRE() macro when we want to free under RCU.
But don't you think  this could be done as a separate change specifically handling this uniformisation effort ?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > this is not the first use of RCU, nor the last one. I think it makes sense to add I'd think it makes sense to add RCU barrier to every module exit.
> 
> Yes, I agree with you Alex, it would be a more generic way to fix rather than the need to decorrelate the OBD_FREE_PRE() macro when we want to free under RCU.
> But don't you think  this could be done as a separate change specifically handling this uniformisation effort ?

sure, absolutely fine with that, so my +1 has been set already :)

parents.subject: LU-17000 utils: Initialize var 'gw' and 'net' before using

commit.subject: LU-17887 obd: do not update obd_memory from RCU
commit.message:
LU-17887 obd: do not update obd_memory from RCU

OBD_FREE_PRE() should not be run from an RCU
callback as the obdclass module may have been
unloaded during the RCU grace period.

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bfaccini@nvidia.com>
Change-Id: I6f663b2aed2e60c15f2a1b9755b2c4050bd91ce2
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/55263
Reviewed-by: Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <jsimmons@infradead.org>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: jenkins <devops@whamcloud.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <maloo@whamcloud.com>
